# Methods: the fitting workflow {#sec-methods-workflow}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

*[Scaffold]* How each model is fitted, checked, compared, and reported — the process every
result in Parts IV–VI passes through.

## Estimation {#sec-methods-workflow-estimation}

*[Scaffold]* NUTS via `nutpie`, with `dev` / `test` / `reporting` sampling presets (the
last is the production configuration); `--target-accept` overrides where a funnel needs it.
*Source:* `scripts/fit_statistical_model.py`, `dse_research_utils` sampling configs.

## Convergence gate {#sec-methods-workflow-convergence}

*[Scaffold]* Convergence is checked **before any estimate is read**: R-hat ≈ 1.00, adequate
bulk and tail ESS, and divergent transitions at or near zero (target < 1%). A non-converged
fit is fixed, not reported. The full diagnostics, including MCSE and a stability check, are
in @sec-computation.

## Predictive checks and model comparison {#sec-methods-workflow-comparison}

*[Scaffold]* Prior- and posterior-predictive checks confirm the likelihood reproduces the
data (@sec-ppc); cross-model comparison uses **PSIS-LOO**, preferring the higher-`elpd`
model only when the difference clears its standard error, with interaction models tested
against their own no-interaction baselines.

## Reporting conventions {#sec-methods-workflow-reporting}

*[Scaffold]* Every estimate is reported as a posterior **median + 95% equal-tailed credible
interval**, with a direction probability and a meaningful-benefit probability against a
ROPE/$\delta$ — **no p-values**. The full conventions, and the frequentist→Bayesian
translation, are in @sec-bridge; the ROPE/$\delta$ registry and evidence ladder in
@sec-priors.

::: {.callout-note title="Two reporting choices, stated explicitly"}
*[Scaffold]* (1) **Bayes factors are not used** — direction and magnitude come from
posterior tail probabilities and a ROPE, not BFs. (2) **Validation:** the status of
fake-data / simulation-based-calibration checks for the model families.
:::
